frameworks.name: maximum-likelihood estimation
frameworks.one_line: reliability-weighted integration of egocentric and allocentric visual cues for reach

frameworks.name: cue combination
frameworks.one_line: multi-cue integration framework motivating reliability-based weighting

frameworks.name: landmark stability heuristic
frameworks.one_line: extrinsic stability of landmarks added as additional weighting factor beyond reliability

core_question: How are egocentric and allocentric visual cues weighted to form memory-guided reach plans, and does landmark stability modulate that weighting beyond cue reliability?
key_assumptions: reach planning integrates egocentric and allocentric cues
reliability of cues determines their MLE weighting
landmark stability provides an additional heuristic signal
key_findings: landmark vibration strongly shifted egocentric-allocentric weighting
vibration did not change pointing variability in control task
MLE model with stability heuristic reproduced the weighting pattern
reduced MLE without stability heuristic failed to capture the effect
interpretations: extrinsic cue stability heuristics matter at least as much as reliability for reach cue weighting
memory-guided reaching uses MLE-like integration augmented by a stability prior
